name: observability
description: kube-prometheus-stack, Grafana, Loki, Thanos, Tempo, dashboards, AlertManager.
sprintGoal: Deploy the full observability stack — Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Thanos, and Tempo — with Ceph-backed storage, Keycloak SSO, and ArgoCD GitOps integration.

stories.title: Helm chart: kube-prometheus-stack
stories.description: Create charts/prometheus-stack/ wrapping prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack. Grafana at grafana.{{ .Values.global.domain }} with Keycloak OIDC. Prometheus retention: 15 days (Thanos handles long-term). AlertManager with a stub for Slack/email routing. Pre-load dashboards: Node Exporter, Kubernetes cluster, GitLab, Ceph (from rook-ceph chart), Istio. Persistent storage on ceph-block.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard ConfigMaps for Node, K8s, GitLab, Ceph, Istio
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Specific score 3: no pinned upstream chart version for kube-prometheus-stack; Keycloak OIDC clientId/secret reference path unspecified; HA gate requirements (PDB, podAntiAffinity, replicaCount>=2) absent from ACs despite being mandatory per CLAUDE.md. Measurable score 3: 'Keycloak OIDC auth for Grafana' is not a binary shell command — needs a concrete check such as `helm template | grep genericOAuth`; 'AlertManager stub config included' should be `helm template | grep -A5 receivers`. Achievable score 3: sourcing 5 external dashboard JSONs (Node Exporter, K8s cluster, GitLab, Ceph, Istio) plus OIDC wiring at 2 points is near budget ceiling — feasible but tight. TimeBound score 4: dashboard sourcing could expand scope; specifying upstream Grafana dashboard IDs (e.g. 1860 for Node Exporter) would tighten the end-state.

stories.title: Helm charts: Loki, Thanos, Tempo
stories.description: Create charts/loki/ wrapping grafana/loki in Simple Scalable mode, storage on ceph-bucket. Create charts/thanos/ wrapping bitnami/thanos with sidecar mode (connects to Prometheus), storage on ceph-bucket. Create charts/tempo/ wrapping grafana/tempo-distributed, storage on ceph-bucket. All pre-configured as Grafana datasources (via ConfigMap in prometheus-stack chart). ArgoCD apps in argocd-apps/observability/.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/thanos/ connects to Prometheus via sidecar
All three auto-registered as Grafana datasources
helm lint passes on all three
argocd-apps/observability/ has all three app manifests
stories.testPlan: Run quality gates: helm lint, shellcheck, kubectl dry-run, verify all acceptanceCriteria.

smart.notes: Specific score 3: three charts with no pinned upstream versions (grafana/loki, bitnami/thanos, grafana/tempo-distributed); HA requirements (PDB, anti-affinity, replicaCount) absent from ACs. Measurable score 3: 'connects to Prometheus via sidecar' needs `helm template | grep -A3 sidecar`; 'All three auto-registered as Grafana datasources' should be `ls charts/prometheus-stack/templates/datasources-*.yaml | wc -l` equals 3. Achievable score 2 (NOT READY): three full Helm wrappers + three ArgoCD apps + three datasource ConfigMaps is too large for one Ralph iteration — split into 026a (Loki), 026b (Thanos), 026c (Tempo), each at 1-2 points. TimeBound score 3: placing datasource ConfigMaps 'in prometheus-stack chart' creates a cross-story dependency that can expand story 025 scope mid-iteration; better to place them in each respective chart's own ConfigMap.
